html:
Model Solution is not the medtech CDMO described in the prior drafts. The cleaner thesis is better: this is a Korean high-fidelity prototype, rapid tooling, injection molding, and small-batch manufacturing platform that is transitioning from project work into repeat production for high-value hardware categories.

The public filings describe a business serving automotive, aerospace, IT/communications, and medical-device customers through Design Prototype, Functional Prototype, IMB, and TSB. The transition matters because IMB and TSB move the company from one-off design validation into manufacturing programs where utilization, automation, and customer stickiness can drive operating leverage.

At roughly ₩21,000 per share, Model Solution’s market cap is about ₩134 billion. The balance sheet is unusually clean for a small manufacturer: 2025 year-end cash plus short-term financial assets were about ₩45.9 billion against ₩11.4 billion of borrowings, leaving roughly ₩34 billion of net financial liquidity. The operating business is therefore valued near ₩100 billion, or about 1.3x 2025 revenue and roughly 12x 2025 EBIT. On a forward view, if 2026 net income reaches ₩8.5-10.0 billion, the ex-cash earnings multiple is closer to 10-12x.

The inflection is already visible. 2025 revenue rose 12.5% to ₩76.5 billion, operating profit rose 92.8% to ₩8.1 billion, and net income rose 29.1% to ₩6.8 billion. The most important line item was IMB, formerly QDM, which grew from ₩18.0 billion in 2024 to ₩30.0 billion in 2025. Exports increased from ₩27.6 billion to ₩37.8 billion, lifting export mix to about 49% of sales. That is not the profile of a local commodity mold shop.

Q1 2026 looked optically soft at only ₩0.7 billion of operating profit on ₩18.7 billion of revenue, but it was still +14% revenue growth and +46% operating-profit growth year over year. The seasonality matters: prototype demand and product-development budgets skew to the second half. TheBell’s May 2026 write-up also identified the right drivers, IMB and Design Prototype growth, medical-device production volume expansion, Amazon and Hyundai collaboration, TSB early-stage costs, and the closing of the U.S. subsidiary to reduce fixed cost.

Variant perception: the market treats Model Solution either as a tiny Korean mold/prototype company or as a retail robotics theme stock. The better lens is “hardware productization infrastructure.” Companies building robots, AR devices, medical tools, consumer electronics, or industrial devices need fast design iteration, CMF, CNC machining, tooling, injection molding, DFM, and low-volume production before they can commit to mass production. Model Solution sits exactly in that pre-mass-production bottleneck, and the customers’ disclosed or reported list includes Samsung, Amazon, Hyundai, and medical-device customers.

The key upside is not that every prototype becomes a huge production run. Most will not. The upside is that a small number of programs can overwhelm the current earnings base. A single successful medical device, diagnostic part, robotics subsystem, AI goggle, or mobility hardware program can pull through tooling, injection molding, assembly, and small-batch recurring production. At ₩76.5 billion of annual revenue, a ₩10-20 billion program is material.

The retail/social angle is useful but should be subordinated to the fundamentals. Korean retail has repeatedly chased robotics and physical-AI names, and Model Solution has been pulled into that stream through Newbility/robotics associations, K-humanoid headlines, and its 2026 Red Dot award for an industrial AI smart goggle. That can create sharp price moves because the float is small, but the investable point is that the theme overlaps with a real operating capability.

The main risk is customer concentration. The 2025 report shows one external customer above 10% of sales at ₩22.7 billion, nearly 30% of revenue, and the company states Samsung Electronics is its largest customer. This is not a diversified SaaS-like revenue base. A delayed device cycle, canceled program, or customer insourcing decision can hurt. TSB is also still proving itself. If it remains a cost center rather than converting to profitable production, the re-rating stalls.

But the downside is cushioned by cash, profitable legacy operations, exports, and a dividend/value-up posture. The company declared a ₩280 dividend and disclosed a 26.2% payout ratio under its 2026 value-up plan. This is not a cash-burning robotics concept stock. It is a profitable micro-cap manufacturer with a clean balance sheet and several embedded call options.

Base case: revenue compounds into ₩90-100 billion over 2026-2027, IMB stays strong, TSB losses moderate, operating margin moves toward 12-13%, and net income reaches ₩9-10 billion. Applying 17-18x earnings to the operating business plus net cash gives a target around ₩32,000, or about +52% from ₩21,000.

Bull case: one or two medical, robotics, industrial AI, or mobility programs convert from development into repeat production, revenue passes ₩120 billion, operating profit reaches ₩17-18 billion, and net income reaches ₩13-14 billion. A 22-24x ex-cash multiple is reasonable for a scarce, profitable hardware-productization platform. Target: ₩55,000, about +162%.

Bear case: IMB growth proves temporary, TSB remains structurally low-margin, Samsung or another large customer slows projects, and the stock is valued as a cyclical mold/prototype shop. Even then, net cash and profitability support value, but the multiple compresses. Target: ₩15,000, about -29%.

Current stock price: roughly ₩21,000. Bear/base/bull targets: ₩15,000, ₩32,000, ₩55,000.
